quick_skim.title: A modular state-space model of human perception, cognition, and decision dynamics
quick_skim.abstract: Human-centered adaptive systems require behavioral models that are both psychologically interpretable and mathematically analyzable. Many existing predictors either operate as black-box input-output mappings or provide limited access to latent internal dynamics. This paper addresses this gap by modeling behavior as a perception-cognition-decision pipeline. We propose a modular state-space model in which attentional selection, predictive inference, cognitive-state evolution, intention formation, and action selection are represented by coupled mathematical mappings. The model links sensory inputs to observable behavior through latent internal states while retaining interpretable connections to neuro-cognitive mechanisms. We establish sufficient conditions for boundedness, Lipschitz regularity, forward invariance, contraction of perceptual inference under constant input, and input-to-state stability of the cognitive state dynamics. Numerical sensitivity analyses show that the model yields interpretable changes in perceptual tracking, cognitive amplification, intention expression, and action decisiveness. We further demonstrate a closed-loop rehabilitation case study in which a receding-horizon controller uses the model to adapt movement difficulty from partial feedback. In this proof-of-concept setting, the model-based controller sustains simulated task participation and achieves lower realized cumulative cost than target-following and random baselines. Overall, the framework provides a white-box dynamical structure for estimation, validation, and model-based control in human-centered settings.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: state-space model for intention formation and decision dynamics
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于意图形成和决策动态的状态空间模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于意图形成和决策动态的状态空间模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于意图形成和决策动态的状态空间模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a modular state-space model integrating attentional selection, predictive inference, and intention formation, interpretable for adaptive systems.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有行为预测模型缺乏可解释性的问题，本文提出模块化状态空间模型，将注意选择、预测推理、意图形成等过程表示为耦合数学映射。模型在保持神经认知可解释性的同时连接感官输入与行为输出。理论分析证明了充分性条件，为实时意图识别提供了可解析框架，有望用于自适应交互系统。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有行为预测模型缺乏可解释性的问题，本文提出模块化状态空间模型，将注意选择、预测推理、意图形成等过程表示为耦合数学映射。模型在保持神经认知可解释性的同时连接感官输入与行为输出。理论分析证明了充分性条件，为实时意图识别提供了可解析框架，有望用于自适应交互系统。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: computational models for understanding human goals through eye movements
